response.step_by_step_log: 1. Analyzed video to identify performance segments. 2. Identified '群青讃歌' at 00:00-04:28 via on-screen text. Performer: Kazama Iroha et al. Original Artist: Eve. 3. Identified '六兆年と一夜物語' at 04:28-05:00 via on-screen text. Performer: Kazama Iroha. Original Artist: kemu. 4. Searched for lyrics for both songs using original artists. 5. Formatted lyrics and timestamps into the required JSON structure.
response.global_summary: The video features a live performance by VTubers (Kazama Iroha, Takane Lui, and others). The first half contains the song '群青讃歌' (Gunjou Sanka) by Eve, and the second half contains the song '六兆年と一夜物語' (Rokuchounen to Ichiya Monogatari) by kemu, performed by Kazama Iroha.

scenes.description: The performers sing and dance to the song '群青讃歌'. The on-screen text identifies the song.

scenes.reference_lyrics_en:
I want to stay connected, that's a believable word,
because we've already said goodbye to the us of yesterday.
A distant bolt of lightning that spent a blue spring,
if I could just cry it out and look only forward,
I wonder how much easier things would have been.
This faint hope that shook my heart,
I've stopped wishing for what I can't have.
An incomplete human,
only this wonderful world holds the answer.
Even if you end up giving up,
there are many regrets waiting for you ahead,
but it's gone now, I won't be lost anymore.
I'll overlap these feelings,
even to the point where I come to love these wounds too.
It's okay to stay imperfect,
run at that pace,
run, let your voice roar.
Hold onto your expectations and anxieties equally,
and light up the future you spin with me now.
We, who don't even know each other's faces or names,
strum a single sound.
A moment like a prank, like a miracle,
time flows by in the blink of an eye.
The fear of continuing to be myself,
I can't take back the time I've lost, but
if I just look back at the past,
it's okay to cry.
Only frustration and pain
will affirm you.
When you become an adult,
will you forget it?
The place where we made our promise,
it's gone now.
I want to remember it,
even to the point where I come to love these memories too.
I'll overlap them,
it's okay to stay imperfect.
That voice will ring out forever,
I've come this far holding onto a certain reason.
It's a journey that hasn't ended yet.
Someday, my feelings will take shape,
covered in this pure white morning,
without being able to forget.
So,
even if you end up giving up,
there are many regrets ahead,
but it's gone now, I won't be lost anymore.
Something irreplaceable,
it overflows.
The answer is in your hands.
Don't let go,
the story takes a step forward.
Even if you end up giving up...

scenes.description: The performer, Kazama Iroha, sings the song '六兆年と一夜物語'. The on-screen text identifies the song and its composer.

scenes.reference_lyrics_en:
In a village from a nameless era,
there was a nameless, young boy.
A fairy tale that no one knows.
From the moment he was born,
he was treated as an ill-omened child, a child of the devil,
and received punishment he couldn't bear.
Although there is nothing sad about it,
when the sunset burns,
my hand is pulled, and...
I don't know, I don't know, I don't know anything.
I don't know the kindness after being scolded,
nor the warmth of hands after the rain.
But honestly, honestly, honestly, honestly, it's so cold.
I won't die, I won't die, why won't I die?
Even though I can't even dream a single dream.
The fairy tale that no one knows,
was sucked into the sunset and disappeared.
With the daily life of violent words,
and contemptuous eyes,
you suddenly stood there.
Even though you shouldn't have spoken to me,
"I want to know your name."
I'm sorry, I don't have a name, nor a tongue.
Even though I have nowhere to belong,
"Let's go home together," my hand is pulled.
I don't know, I don't know, I don't know anything.
That you are no longer a child too,
I don't know the warmth of a stranger's hand,
nor the kindness after being scolded,
but honestly, honestly, honestly, honestly, it's so cold.
I won't die, I won't die, why won't I die?
Even though I can't even dream a single dream.
The fairy tale that no one knows,
was sucked into the sunset and disappeared.
